Notes: First time pulling on tufas! Pretty lame, rotten start that led to some fun movement higher up. Pretty worked from TMZ and biffed it two feet shy of the anchors, but I'm stoked to have almost onsighted this 12a after climbing 2300ft!

Started climbing at 6:23, 5h19m to summit with a poo break at the bivy ledge, then 20 minutes chilling at the summit, hit the ground at 2:52 for a total time of 8h30m. What a wild romp! We simuled in blocks of 4 standard pitches each, Taylor started us off. Taylor led the last block to below the crux pitch, which I took and linked with the next, then Taylor took us to the summit! Saw a cool falcon about midway up, some GORGEOUS sunbeams over the valley as the sun rose, and a funny stuck drill bit at the bivy ledge. I was pretty happy to lead a 10d pitch at the top of one of my blocks. Definitely a 5-star route, so awesome!! We had a funny situation rappelling when our rope wasn't long enough, so Taylor went to the ground and we extended my side of the rope and I switched to his while he stayed clipped in to the other side for a counterbalance. Love staying safe and working it out :)

Climb: When You're Lost In The Wild
Notes: Swung & linked pitches. I took 3, 6&7, and 10&11. So chossy!! Hopefully the choss on the actual climbing will clear up with time, but some of the belays were on straight gravel that will probably never go away. There are still some pretty big death blocks chilling out up there.

I took P1 and Taylor took P2. Pretty fun movement, awesome setting! Chris crashed his drone from the other spire yikessss. A Canadian woman rapped on a loose hold on her way up and we felt it all the way at the top :skull-emoji
Basically the EPC version of Starry hahaha. So many fun pockets and such fun movement! The crux hit me like a sack of bricks. I might be able to get it if I came back fresh!

I led 2 and 5. Definitely a classic for a reason! The crux had me goin', but I managed to pull through and keep the pump away. Thank goodness we were the first crew that showed up, the next party was a bit of a mess! Chatted with Newell on speakerphone while belaying Taylor up P5 and while I climbed P6.

What a wild romp! 7h46m total, 5h24m to the summit, 2h22m rappelling. We linked a ton of pitches (&). I took 3&4, 6&7, 12&13, and 14. Only took two falls following 15, but man is it stout and sharp. No rattlesnakes to speak of, and Chris got some cool drone shots of Taylor leading 10&11! Insane exposure on 14 and 15 holy snoot.

Partner(s): Chris Co., Colin Mc., Ashley Ac., Taylor Be.
Notes: Night climb, pretty fun line! I thought this pitch was more fun than P1 of Yankee Clipper.

Partner(s): Chris Co., Colin Mc., Ashley Ac., Taylor Be.
Notes: Climbed this through the sunset and needed a headlamp by the time I touched the ground!

Swung leads, I took the last pitch since Taylor linked 1 and 2. The crux was super techy and fun, but I got the money pitch which was sustained 10a pretty much the whole time with a super sick topout! Chris got some sweet drone footage towards the top too
Fun bolted crack climb, it was fun to cruise through all the jams while clipping bolts!

So sick!!! New hardest onsight! Super techy crux halfway up to traverse past the mid anchors that should not be missed, then a tough heartbreaker physical crux right at the end. There was a lizard guardian at the high crux who watched me figure out how to get there, then scampered into the crux undercling and I thought he would bite me for sure but he didn't.
Sick climb, lives up to the hype! I thought pitch 3 was the money pitch, not pitch 4. It was such a fun, cruiser romp with really fun, sustained climbing! Swung leads, I took odds
